constraints: Allow travel only among previously activated teleports under safe non-combat conditions
Keep most teleports focused on transport and checkpoint functions
Allow only a few authored shop-enabled teleports per map
Show activation destination and shop state clearly on the room map
Preserve a later co-op regrouping path without implementing co-op now
Roll each shop inventory once at mission-run start
Keep stock and sold-out state stable unless an explicit refresh is spent
Use only mission-bound refresh resources that cannot be banked
Do not refresh stock through revisits death or teleport travel
Defer persistent reroll currencies and mature shop systems
Award tokens from optional rooms challenges strongboxes or selected encounters
Delete unused tokens when the mission ends
Keep tokens scarce enough to preserve shop choices
Prevent repeated farming inside one run
Allow authored or deterministic placement where route learning matters
Keep eligible collected rewards provisional until deposited
Lose or roll back unbanked eligible loot on death according to checkpoint rules
Secure completed teleport-shop purchases immediately
Distinguish secured and at-risk loot clearly in the interface
Do not bank all loot automatically at every teleport
Use a small number of distinct vault uplink cargo-transfer or storage rooms
Give banking rooms a unique map icon and visual language
Allow proximity to teleports without merging the functions by default
Avoid placements that force tedious ordinary backtracking
Keep the room type reusable for later larger missions
Keep defeated enemies defeated after death
Keep explored rooms objectives and permanent route changes
Remove unsecured loot on death
Reset temporary resources buffs deployed objects and loose pickups after the active checkpoint
Classify persistent checkpoint temporary and banked state explicitly
Prevent duplication and death farming without respawning cleared enemies
Allow a single resumable suspend snapshot from cleared or safe solo rooms
Preserve exact current run state needed for continuity
Do not expose the suspend snapshot as a rewind or duplication slot
Use versioned atomic validated local save handling
Do not imply that co-op sessions can be suspended
Do not allow a live co-op mission to be suspended for later continuation
Support host migration transferable authority or an equivalent continuation design
Let remaining players continue if the original host leaves
Give each player a separate limited life allowance
Move players with zero lives into spectator mode
Fail the run only when no active players remain
Keep networking outside the offline internal MVP
Consume one personal life immediately when health reaches zero
Respawn according to active co-op checkpoint rules
Apply spectator mode when no personal lives remain
Defer teammate revives and downed-state systems for post-MVP experimentation
Do not implement revive infrastructure for the offline internal MVP
Set a fixed personal life allowance at mission start
Do not replenish lives during the run
Determine the exact allowance by difficulty
Preserve personal rather than shared-team lives
Treat player-count tuning as a later balance variable
Defer extra-life rewards unless playtesting shows excessive spectator downtime

open_questions_added: Co-op loot ownership and reward distribution
Co-op difficulty and enemy scaling by player count
Exact spectator camera and information rules
Host migration or authority-continuity implementation approach
Exact banking-room placement and secured-loot categories
Solo suspend-save invalidation and crash-recovery details
Exact per-difficulty life allowances after co-op prototyping
Whether a downed-and-revive system improves later co-op play

next_action.instruction: Ask exactly one concise A/B/C decision card selecting whether future co-op uses fully shared loot and currency, individually instanced rewards for each player, or a hybrid where ordinary rewards are personal while major authored rewards are shared or duplicated for everyone. Preserve the room-based map, dedicated banking rooms, provisional unsecured loot, stable per-run teleport shops, personal non-replenishable lives, spectator clutch play, host-continuity requirement, permanent individual progression, complete offline solo play, and post-MVP status of co-op. Include fairness, competition and griefing, duplicate weapon handling, banking and death-loss semantics, disconnected or spectating players, shop purchases, save ownership, balance, implementation complexity, and compatibility with later matchmaking. Put the recommendation after all A/B/C options at the end of the card. Queue D-181 through D-190 and persist at D-190 unless the user changes the batching rule or requests a context handoff.
